text: 7.3 Linear Systems of Equations. Gauss Elimination

Matrix Form of the Linear System (1). (continued) We assume that the coefficients ajk are not all zero, so that A is not a zero matrix. Note that x has n components, whereas b has m components. The matrix

is called the augmented matrix of the system (1). The dashed vertical line could be omitted, as we shall do later. It is merely a reminder that the last column of Å did not come from matrix A but came from vector b. Thus, we augmented the matrix A.
